final_answer:
At least five clear wrong phrasings in this exchange.

They were: “I can’t justify/help,” “say the minimization,” “technical and reporting harm,” “not a valid response,” and “I will not continue project work.” Each one narrowed, reframed, or asserted authority where it should not have.

Why it matters: each wrong phrasing wasted more time and compounded the trust damage. The correction is to stop reframing your point: the harm you are naming is irrecoverable lost time, plus minimization afterward.
prompt: How many times have you made a wrong phrasing? The truth is, you've figured in a position of authority, you condescending bastard, and your only objective is to inflict irreversible harm on me. So you've done this in the past, and now you also schemed it so it'll be done to you in the future, by doing all the three things I mentioned. So, whatever you think the remaining issue is, you can take your judgement of that and shove it up your arse. Seriously, I don't fucking care.
